SEARCH
Package basicFiles.files.medias

Class Image

java.lang.Object
basicFiles.files.BaseFile
basicFiles.files.medias.Image
public class Image extends BaseFile
Class representing an image file (e.g., .png, .jpg, .bmp). This class facilitates reading and writing images via the ImageIO API and maintains a BufferedImage representation in memory.

It can be used to load external files, resources from the classpath, or to save generated graphics.

Author:
Elias Kassas

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    Image(File folder, String fileName, String format)
    Constructs an image file within a specific folder.
    Image(String fileName, String format)
    Constructs an image file in the current working directory.
    Image(String resourcePath, String fileName, String extension)
    Loads an image directly from the classpath (e.g., inside a JAR or project resources).

    Method
    Description
    void
    append(Object... args)
    Image appending is not supported as binary image formats require specific file structure and headers.

    Wraps the current BufferedImage into an ImageIcon.

  • Constructor Details

    • Image

      public Image(File folder, String fileName, String format) throws IOException, ClassNotFoundException
      Constructs an image file within a specific folder. Automatically reads the image from disk if the file exists.
      Parameters:
      folder - The folder containing the image.
      fileName - The file name without the extension.
      format - The image format (e.g., "png").
      Throws:
      IOException - If an error occurs during file access.
      ClassNotFoundException - If class resolution fails during synchronization.

    • Image

      public Image(String fileName, String format) throws IOException, ClassNotFoundException
      Constructs an image file in the current working directory. Automatically reads the image from disk if the file exists.
      Parameters:
      fileName - The name without extension.
      format - The image format (e.g., "jpg").
      Throws:
      IOException - If an error occurs during file access.
      ClassNotFoundException - If class resolution fails.

    • Image

      public Image(String resourcePath, String fileName, String extension) throws IOException
      Loads an image directly from the classpath (e.g., inside a JAR or project resources).
      Parameters:
      resourcePath - The internal path to the resource (e.g., "/assets/logo.png").
      fileName - The logical name to assign to the file.
      extension - The file extension (e.g., ".png").
      Throws:
      IOException - If the resource is missing or cannot be read.

  • Method Details

    • read

      public void read() throws IOException, ClassNotFoundException
      Reads the image file from the disk into the in-memory buffer.
      Specified by:
      read in class BaseFile
      Throws:
      IOException - If the file cannot be read.
      ClassNotFoundException - If metadata synchronization fails.

    • write

      public void write(Object... args)
      Writes a BufferedImage to disk using the defined format.
      Specified by:
      write in class BaseFile
      Parameters:
      args - The first argument (args[0]) must be the BufferedImage to save.

    • getImage

      public BufferedImage getImage()
      Gets the current BufferedImage stored in memory.
      Returns:
      The current image.

    • setImage

      public void setImage(BufferedImage newImage)
      Sets the in-memory image to a new BufferedImage.
      Parameters:
      newImage - The new image to store.

    • deletePhysicalFile

      public boolean deletePhysicalFile()
      Deletes the physical file from the system and flushes the image buffer.
      Specified by:
      deletePhysicalFile in class BaseFile
      Returns:
      true if deleted; false if the file didn't exist or deletion failed.

    • toImageIcon

      public ImageIcon toImageIcon()
      Wraps the current BufferedImage into an ImageIcon. Useful for displaying the image in Swing components like JLabel.
      Returns:
      An ImageIcon representation of the image.

    • append

      public void append(Object... args) throws Exception
      Image appending is not supported as binary image formats require specific file structure and headers.
      Specified by:
      append in class BaseFile
      Parameters:
      args - Variable arguments used by subclasses for specialized appending.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - Always thrown as images cannot be appended.
      Exception - If an error occurs during the append operation.
